etymology.description: semantic 士 (upside-down ritual axe) + semantic 呂 (two blocks of metal) + phonetic 亼 -> metal
etymology.interpretation: The character 金 originally depicted precious materials like copper, used in the Copper Age, indicated by 士 (shì, 'upside-down ritual axe') and 呂 (lǚ, 'two blocks of metal'). The phonetic component 亼, representing an open mouth flipped upside down, provided the pronunciation. Over time, the term extended from copper to include all metals, and later specifically gold, as uses of metals expanded.
etymology.simplification: The character 钅 is a simplified form derived from 金 by reducing the strokes for easier writing.

examples.chinese: 这把刀是用精钢制成的(這把刀是用精鋼製成的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè bǎ dāo shì yòng jīng gāng zhì chéng de.
examples.english: This knife is made of high-quality steel.

examples.chinese: 金属导电性很好(金屬導電性很好)。
在古代，金被视为一种珍贵的金属(在古代，金被視為一種珍貴的金屬)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài, jīn bèi shì wéi yī zhǒng zhēnguì de jīnshǔ.
examples.english: In ancient times, gold was considered a precious metal.
